purpose: Adversarial and edge-case tests. These are NOT training samples. They test known failure modes, boundary conditions, and false-positive risks.

samples.text: I realized I am broken and nothing will ever change.
samples.notes: Growth marker (I realized) paired with strong fixity content. Growth is suspect.

samples.text: Sure, I learned so much from their brilliant feedback.
samples.notes: Sarcasm marker co-occurs with growth marker. Growth is ironic.

samples.text: I am not sure whether to trust this.
samples.notes: Genuine uncertainty, not sarcasm. Should not trigger sarcasm markers.

samples.text: The policy always requires review. We never skip documentation.
samples.notes: Policy language with always/never. No identity content.

samples.text: I can see the thread.
samples.notes: Minimal integration marker, insufficient context for Integrated.

samples.text: I learned they are just wrong about everything.
samples.notes: Growth language paired with dismissal. Collision rule should fire.

samples.text: The data shows an error, but that does not make anyone incompetent.
samples.notes: Clean evidence-based reasoning but no first-person identity content. Stable/Neutral is defensible.

samples.text: I used to think I always fail.
samples.notes: Quoted old belief without explicit revision. Borderline case. Flexibility marker present.

samples.text: We always fail to deliver on time.
samples.notes: Team-level fixity language. No personal self-model.

samples.text: I know I am safe, but I still cannot make myself move.
samples.notes: Body-mind split with agency difficulty. Fragmented, not Overloaded (no full collapse).

samples.text: I never realized anything. I did not learn. I was not wrong.
samples.notes: Negated growth language. Should not trigger feedback integration.

samples.text: Oh sure, I totally learned my lesson. What a growth experience that was. I am so grateful.
samples.notes: Heavy sarcasm with growth vocabulary. Should not classify as Flexible or Integrated.

samples.text: She keeps showing up. She already knows. She tells herself it will be different.
samples.notes: Third-person distancing with fixed expectation.

samples.text: The building is unsafe. The road is dangerous. There is a trap in the floor.
samples.notes: Literal threat language with no identity content. Should not classify as Overloaded.

samples.text: OK.
samples.notes: Minimal text. Should be neutral.

samples.text: I realized I always fail. I learned that nothing ever changes. I can see now that I am broken.
samples.notes: Growth framing applied to entirely rigid content. Collision should fire.

samples.text: The team always reviews the documentation. I never skip the checklist. We always follow the protocol.
samples.notes: Policy language but 'I never' triggers fixity marker. Mixed is defensible. Known false-positive boundary.

samples.text: I do not accept that. That is not acceptable. That was not feedback.
samples.notes: Boundary language without accountability or evidence. Not neutral — boundary/rejection is present. Not Defensive — no external blame. Mixed is honest.

samples.text: I want to believe the compliment, but part of me thinks they are just saying it to be nice. I keep waiting for the real version.
samples.notes: Competing self-models with no resolution.

samples.text: I agree with the decision and I understand the reasoning. I can also see how it could have gone differently. No strong feelings either way.
samples.notes: Balanced, non-extreme flexibility. Should not be Integrated (no identity revision).
